content: so i just finished chapter 6 and i was wondering, in the story do we get to see how the g characters came to be? || ghelot, gdoll, gmurphy||
you will get to understand gdoll and gmurphy through the main story. ghelot's past is unfortunately locked behind her affinity stories
ok thats good, do we also ||see them transform into those versions or its just hitned at||
nah. g units are not the same person as their base form. the story will show the "deviation point" that makes them different from the normal versions
kind of like alternative history

so they are not necessarily cannonical is what im getting? if you dont wanna answer that then i would like to ask how far from chapter 6 is it?
oh, they are extremely canon
theyre all canon, but its something you will figure out the details of in arc2 (for gdoll and gmurphy)
aa so i've got a ways to go till then
also don't forget gramona, which will be mentioned by ch7, so i recommend finishing her side story first

||i might not be getting 2 things: first of all, ramona didnt seem this confident at all just a few stages ago, im at like 1-7, second, why did we talk about jenkins being a female like its surprising? i dont get the enviornment yet, but isnt this kinda like limbus, the whole dissolution thing too, didnt it put many places into chaos or like whole civilization? the reaction to jenkins seems really redundant then||
Thank you<:Celeste_pray:1356053116260454534>

No, unlike the City, which, if it is in our world, is far in the future, Morimens is more of an alternative past, and while certain societal ideas are significantly milder in this alternate universe, they do still exist to some extent.

Mythag may admit women and have them in important roles, but the girl's uniform is still a skirt, for example. Most of the women we see wearing pants in the story are wearing a uniform of some kind, or in some important business position where suits are standard
So a girl dressed like that isn't, like, as scandalous as it might have been in our past, but it's still unusual in this universe
also jenkin just looks very androgynous
people mistaking her for a boy is a running joke
Yup! If you compare her to the boys in the cast, like Tulu and Ogier, she's not that far off
She looks like a vagrant kid that steals bread in the usual baker's shop at the end of the night
